title: Comparatives and Superlatives
objectives: Structure short sentences using comparative and superlative forms to describe cities and environments
Write short sentences using comparative and superlative forms to describe cities and environments

key_concepts.explanation: We use the comparative to compare two things, and the superlative to compare three or more. Three rules based on adjective type:
Add -er (comparative) / -est (superlative). Examples: tall → taller → tallest, smart → smarter → smartest.
Change y → i and add -er / -est. Examples: happy → happier → happiest.
Use more (comparative) / most (superlative). Examples: crowded → more crowded → most crowded, beautiful → more beautiful → most beautiful.
A syllable has one vowel sound. smart = 1 syllable, crow/ded = 2 syllables, beau/ti/ful = 3 syllables. Vowel reminder: a, e, i, o, u.

exercises.problem: Complete with the correct comparative or superlative: 1. A whale is ___ (large) than a dolphin. 2. Sarah's room is ___ (clean) in the house. 3. The new bag is ___ (heavy) than my old one. 4. Pollution is one of the ___ (serious) problems on Earth. 5. This building is ___ (modern) than the one across the street.
exercises.answer: 1. larger, 2. the cleanest, 3. heavier, 4. most serious, 5. more modern

exercises.problem: Read about Hamad's City and complete with adjectives (busy, quiet, dangerous, fast): Hamad lives in Kuwait City. He thinks his city is becoming ___ than before. The streets are more dangerous now because there are more cars. The new park near his house is one of the ___ places to visit. The new buses are ___ and more modern than the old ones. He sometimes misses how quiet the city used to be, but he likes the new changes.
Write About Your Place: Write three sentences about your city, school, or environment. Use comparatives and superlatives to describe how things are changing or improving. Share with a partner.
exercises.answer: Open-ended using words like beautiful, safe, clean, big, warm

teaching_notes: Grammar lesson on comparatives and superlatives. Grammar Box table with three rules (short/y-ending/long adjectives). Syllable guide included. Think-Pair-Share with three city pictures (A, B, C). Practice: controlled fill-in → cloze passage about Kuwait City → free writing about own place. 1 page.
